title: Fixing Invalid Git Branch Name Validation

statement.markdown: Write a JavaScript function to validate Git branch names. A branch name is valid if it only contains alphanumeric characters, dots, hyphens, and underscores. Write a function that takes a string as input and returns a boolean indicating whether the branch name is valid or not.
statement.inputFormat: The function will be called with a string representing the branch name.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a boolean indicating whether the branch name is valid or not.
statement.notes: The function should handle empty strings and null inputs correctly.

examples.explanation: Empty string is not a valid branch name
Invalid branch name with leading slash

hints.text: First hint text: Start by checking for invalid characters like slashes and special characters.

editorial.title: Fixing Invalid Git Branch Name Validation
editorial.intuition: The function should use a regular expression to match valid characters.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a loop to check each character in the string.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a regular expression to match valid characters.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to match valid characters and check for an empty string.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return true for valid branch names and false for invalid branch names.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not handling empty strings correctly.
Mistake 2: Not using a regular expression to match valid characters.
